attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- lightfast-changelog: Write and review AEO-optimized changelog entries for Lightfast. Triggers when the user asks
to write a changelog entry, review changelog content, or asks about changelog structure,
AEO optimization, or release notes formatting.
- lightfast-sdk: Use Lightfast tools to search across team knowledge and interact with connected providers
(GitHub, Linear, Sentry, Vercel, Apollo). Triggers when the user asks about errors, issues,
PRs, deployments, or wants to search across their dev tools. Also triggers proactively when
investigating bugs, reviewing code, or checking deployment status.
- lightfast-inngest: Inspect Inngest function runs, debug failures, send events, and invoke functions via the
local Inngest dev server MCP. Triggers when the user asks about background jobs, workflow
runs, failed steps, event delivery, or wants to invoke/test Inngest functions.
- lightfast-db: Query and inspect the Postgres database schema and data. Triggers when the user asks about
tables, columns, rows, database schema, wants to run a SELECT query, or needs to understand
the data model. Read-only — cannot modify data.
- turborepo: Turborepo monorepo build system guidance. Triggers on: turbo.json, task pipelines,
dependsOn, caching, remote cache, the "turbo" CLI, --filter, --affected, CI optimization, environment
variables, internal packages, monorepo structure/best practices, and boundaries.

Use when user: configures tasks/workflows/pipelines, creates packages, sets up
monorepo, shares code between apps, runs changed/affected packages, debugs cache,
or has apps/packages directories.
- implement_plan: Implement technical plans from thoughts/shared/plans with verification
- create_plan: Create detailed implementation plans through interactive research and iteration
- validate_plan: Validate implementation against plan, verify success criteria, identify issues
- commit: Create git commits with user approval and no Claude attribution
- oneshot_merge: Commit changes, create PR, and merge into main in one shot
- improve_plan: Adversarial review of implementation plans — find weaknesses, kill code smells, improve DX
- research_codebase: Document codebase as-is with thoughts directory for historical context
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
No type errors. The landing page now has the manifesto section (text + shader + commit link) inserted between the hero hairline and FAQ. The page is async to support the `getLatestCommit()` fetch. The manifesto section uses `className="dark"` to force dark theme like the original company layout did.

Do you want me to also delete the old company page now that it's been merged into the landing page, or keep it around for now?

message.content: hmm for some reason the shader is not showing

content.text: Let me check if the `dark` class on the section might be causing issues, and look at how the shader renders.

The shader uses `width: "100%"` and `height: "100%"` — it inherits its size from the parent. In the original company page, the parent was `flex-[3]` inside a `flex h-screen flex-col` container, giving it a concrete height. In the landing page, the parent `div` with `flex-[3]` is inside a `min-h-screen` flex column, but `min-h-screen` doesn't give a fixed height for flex children to divide.

The issue is that `min-h-screen` doesn't establish a definite height for flex basis calculation. The shader's `height: 100%` collapses to 0.

content.text: Changed `min-h-screen` to `h-screen` on the manifesto container. The original company page used `h-screen` — with `min-h-screen`, the flex children (`flex-[5]` and `flex-[3]`) have no definite height to divide, so the shader's `height: 100%` collapsed to 0. Should be visible now.

content.text: No type errors. Replaced the WebGL shader with a canvas flow field — Perlin noise drives the angle of ~600 curves that fade in/out, with a slight directional bias (`BASE_ANGLE`). Black background, muted warm lines (`rgba(200, 210, 180, ...)`), gentle trail fade. Take a look and let me know if you want to adjust the density, colors, curve direction, or speed.
I'll rewrite it to draw once on mount — no animation loop, no particle lifecycle.

content.content: The file /Users/jeevanpillay/Code/@lightfastai/lightfast/apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/(landing)/_components/flow-field.tsx has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.text: Now reads `--background` and `--border` CSS variables from the canvas element at draw time, so it respects the dark theme context.
The CSS variables might use `oklch` or raw values without `hsl()` wrapping. Let me check the theme format.
`oklch`, not `hsl`. Wrapping with `hsl()` produces invalid color → falls back to black. I'll use `oklch()` instead.

  open <url>                 Navigate to URL
  click <sel>                Click element (or @ref)
  dblclick <sel>             Double-click element
  type <sel> <text>          Type into element
  fill <sel> <text>          Clear and fill
  press <key>                Press key (Enter, Tab, Control+a)
  keyboard type <text>       Type text with real keystrokes (no selector)
  keyboard inserttext <text> Insert text without key events
  hover <sel>                Hover element
  focus <sel>                Focus element
  check <sel>                Check checkbox
  uncheck <sel>              Uncheck checkbox
  select <sel> <val...>      Select dropdown option
  drag <src> <dst>           Drag and drop
  upload <sel> <files...>    Upload files
  download <sel> <path>      Download file by clicking element
  scroll <dir> [px]          Scroll (up/down/left/right)
  scrollintoview <sel>       Scroll element into view
  wait <sel|ms>              Wait for element or time
  screenshot [path]          Take screenshot
  pdf <path>                 Save as PDF
  snapshot                   Accessibility tree with refs (for AI)
  eval <js>                  Run JavaScript
  connect <port|url>         Connect to browser via CDP

content.text:
The flow field canvas is rendering and the colors are correct. But the manifesto text section with `flex-[5]` is taking up a huge amount of space (it uses absolute positioning so it has no intrinsic content height), and the canvas (`flex-[3]`) is also very tall. The `h-screen` container makes the whole thing 100vh, but the text section with absolute children collapses — so the canvas is getting most of the space.

The issue is that the text content inside `flex-[5]` is absolutely positioned, giving it zero intrinsic height, which means the flex ratio doesn't work as expected. Let me check the manifesto section layout and fix the sizing.

content.text:
I see — the `h-screen` flex column with `flex-[5]` / `flex-[3]` is working, but visually the manifesto text is invisible (absolutely positioned off to the right at `left-[50%]`) and the flow field canvas at `flex-[3]` is getting 37.5% of the viewport height. The canvas itself looks fine — it's rendering with the right colors. But the overall section is `h-screen` tall with the text occupying 62.5% and the canvas 37.5%.

It seems like the sizing is actually working as designed from the original company page layout. What specifically looks wrong to you — is the canvas too tall, too short, or is the manifesto text not visible? Let me scroll to see the full section.

input.command: agent-browser scroll up 400 2>&1 && sleep 1 && agent-browser screenshot /tmp/manifesto-top.png 2>&1

lastPrompt: use agent-browser, the sizing of the canvas container doesnt seem to be correct.

content.text:
Now I can see the issue clearly. The manifesto text area (`flex-[5]`) is mostly empty space — the text is pushed off-screen to the right with `absolute left-[50%]`, and the "Read the Program" / "We are building the runtime" row is at the bottom. The manifesto body text isn't visible in the viewport at all because it's absolutely positioned starting at `left: 50%` and going off the right edge.

The canvas flow field at the bottom is rendering correctly with the right colors and curves. The real problem is the manifesto text layout — `absolute left-[50%]` without a `transform: translateX(-50%)` pushes it to start at the horizontal midpoint, which works on the original full-screen company page but here the text overflows.

But you said the canvas sizing doesn't seem correct — looking at it, the canvas section seems appropriately sized at ~37% of viewport height. Is the issue that it's too tall/short, or was it the manifesto text layout you were referring to?

message.content: its more like 75% the viewport? or am i tripping

content.text: Let me measure the actual canvas dimensions.

input.command: agent-browser eval "const canvas = document.querySelector('canvas'); const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect(); JSON.stringify({ canvasHeight: rect.height, viewportHeight: window.innerHeight, ratio: (rect.height / window.innerHeight * 100).toFixed(1) + '%' })" 2>&1

content.content: "{\"canvasHeight\":640,\"viewportHeight\":577,\"ratio\":\"110.9%\"}"

content.text:
You're not tripping — the canvas is 641px tall in a 577px viewport, over 100% of viewport height. The `flex-[3]` out of 8 total should give it 37.5% of `h-screen`, but it's way oversized. The issue is that `flex-[5]` has only absolutely positioned children, so it has zero intrinsic content height and collapses, letting `flex-[3]` stretch to fill almost all of the `h-screen`.

I'll give the flow field a fixed proportion instead of relying on flex.
